reasoning: The baseline value as of Nov 9, 2025 is 0.40%. Savings deposit rates are sticky but generally move in tandem with the Federal Funds rate. Recent market consensus (as of late October 2025) strongly expects the Federal Reserve to continue cutting its policy rate by 25 basis points at the October and December meetings, aiming for around 3.6% by year-end 2025 and lower in 2026. Because the Fed is in an active easing cycle, banks face no competitive or economic pressure to raise the interest rates they pay on savings accounts. In fact, many institutions lower their yields immediately after a Fed rate cut. Consequently, the national average savings rate (which has been flat at 0.40% for the past three months following a slight recovery from a June drop) is highly likely to remain flat or decrease as the latest and upcoming Federal Reserve cuts take effect in the banking system. Short-term bounces upward are unlikely but possible due to noise in the survey/weightings, so the probability is kept modestly above zero, increasing slightly for the later resolution date due to inherent forecasting uncertainty over longer horizons.
